response:
From our help center on this topic:
ATM kept my card / I forgot my PIN
ATMs commonly capture (retain) a Visa card after three consecutive
incorrect PIN attempts. The captured card stays in the ATM until the
operating bank retrieves and disposes of it during the next
maintenance cycle (usually within 1–2 business days).
Immediate steps
1. Note the ATM details — bank name, branch, ATM ID, date/time.
2. Call your card issuer using the number on the back of your card
to confirm the PIN block and block the card from further use.
3. If you cannot reach the issuer, call Visa Global Customer Assistance
([phone_redacted]

From our help center on this topic:
Managing your DevPlatform workspace subscription
Workspace owners can manage seats, plan tier, and billing settings.
Pausing a subscription
You can pause a workspace subscription when you have temporarily
stopped hiring. While paused:
- Existing candidates retain access to in-flight tests.
- You cannot send new invites.
- Billing is suspended for the pause window.
To pause, open Settings > Billing > Pause subscription and choose a
pause length (1, 3, or 6 months). At the end of the window the
subscription resumes automatically.

Disadvantages and limitations

response:
Here's what our documentation says:
Adding extra time (time accommodation) for candidates
You can grant extra time to one or more candidates for a specific test.
Time is added as a percentage of the base test duration; the
configured percentage must be in multiples of 5 (e.g. 25%, 50%, 75%).
Steps to add time accommodation
1. Log in to your DevPlatform for Work account.
2. Open the Tests tab and select the test.
3. Open the Candidates tab.
4. Tick the checkbox next to the candidate(s) needing accommodation.
5. Click More > Add Time Accommodation.
6. Enter the accommodation percentage (multiples of 5).
7.

Opting your website out of crawling by 
web crawler identifies itself by its user-agent. To opt your
website out of crawling, add a robots.txt rule that disallows the
crawler.
Robots.txt example
```
User-agent: -ai
Disallow: /
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /
```
Use both user-agent names — crawler has used both
identifiers historically.

response:
Based on the documented process, here's how this works:
Claude API rate limits
Rate limits are applied per organization and per model. They are expressed
in three dimensions: requests per minute (RPM), input tokens per minute
(ITPM), and output tokens per minute (OTPM).
When you hit a rate limit, the API returns HTTP 429 with a
retry-after header indicating how long to wait before retrying.
How to avoid 429s
- Implement exponential backoff with jitter on 429 responses.
- Cache results when a request is idempotent.
- For batch workloads, use the Files / Batches APIs which run against a
separate, higher rate limit.

Here's what our documentation says:
Conversation and workspace data isolation in Claude
Each Claude account has its own private conversation history. Other
users — including members of your workspace, family-shared Apple/Google
accounts, or other people who use the same browser profile after signing
out — cannot see your conversations.
What is NOT shared
- Personal conversation history between Claude accounts.
- Uploaded files between separate accounts.
- Project instructions or memories between accounts.
What IS shared (when you opt in)
- Conversations explicitly shared via the Share link feature.
- Files added

response:
From our help center on this topic:
Deleting a conversation removes it from your account and queues it for
permanent deletion from storage according to the retention
schedule published on privacy.claude.com. Uploaded files attached to the
conversation are also deleted as part of this process.
If you used the conversation for sensitive content, also consider using
Temporary Chat going forward — these conversations are not saved to
your history.
Reference: https://privacy.claude.com/en/articles/[phone_redacted]-how-can-i-delete-or-rename-a-conversation

imposing a minimum-purchase amount for credit-card transactions**.

credit card transactions under federal law — and that minimum
cannot apply to debit cards.
